summary: Recast the opening paragraph so Chapter 4 no longer enters on the same time-and-dust scaffold visible in the Chapter 5 boundary excerpt, while preserving August's early arrival, the bank's sensory field, and his procedural cast of mind. The Vale-yard ending already handed Cora's ten-day notice cleanly into the next chapter, so it was left intact. No style-bible example_lines appeared verbatim in the chapter.
edits: Reordered the opening so it begins with August's fixation on bank air and numbers before moving into the early-morning bank entry.
